reviews.author: Mrs A
reviews.text:
Nice little forest walk with clearly mapped trails, including some longer and more challenging options. There’s plenty of free parking and lots of picnic benches scattered throughout.

The area has lots of tall trees and a few small waterfalls, and the hill itself is a steady climb rather than steep. We did the Vinnalls Loop with two children aged 10 and 12 and they managed it without any problems. It might be a little on the boring side for some kids as there aren’t many child-focused features

reviews.author: John R
reviews.text:
Enjoyed a really nice woodland walk using the 2 mile red path, the place has amazing scenery and was very peaceful.

This is a woodland walk I would highly recommend.

I would have give 5 stars, but, the only disappointment was the amount of poo bags thrown or left behind by dog walkers, really pathetic that these owners bag them and then leave them scattered all over the forest.

Some blame need to lie with the staff running the site, they have a huge office and multiple cars so plenty of staff

reviews.author: Laura Jurkeviciute
reviews.text:
A great forest to explore for a day out. There are paths and different routes to take. We parked at Vinnalls car park, it's a spacious car park and while there were a handful of cars, we barely saw any people - it wasn't overcrowded.
If you're taking a reactive dog out, the main paths are wide enough to make space.
Really enjoyed visiting and will come back again.

places_data.editorial_summary: Ramshackle ruins of an 11th-century castle dismantled to prevent its use in the English Civil War.

Very overgrown which is understandable from a nature point of view but it means a lot of the ruins can not be seen. Still worth a visit.

Had a brilliant day out. Plenty of options for which trail you want to follow.
Brilliant smaller trails if you have active children and a park near the car park.
We chose the 7km which wasn't just a flat paved trail, there were a lot of slopes and various terrains, it was also a lot quieter; we only saw 2 other people. The 7km could also do with a few more arrows as we did go off trail a couple of times, but not an issue for us as we love adventures.
The long trail took us 2hrs on a slow walk. T

reviews.author: Chloe Tate
reviews.text: Plenty to do here in terms of walks/cycling. They have some smaller family friendly walks themed around the gruffalo /stick man. You can purchase little activity packs foe the stick man trial at the visitor centre,  I believe for £5, I'd say they were more suited to older toddlers 5+ personally. A few different parks located around the area and some  picnic benches. Will definitely be returning.

reviews.author: Stephen Briggs
reviews.text: A great pace to visit the great outdoors there is off road parking and routes to follow. In shooting season you may find a pheasant shooting on.

reviews.author: Nicola
reviews.text: Great forest lots of routes to explore. You can follow the forestry roads or go off into the trees for a wilder adventure. Lots of wildlife and mushrooms everywhere. Great quiet place for a walk theres some spots with some lovely views across the country side. A few different parking spots available. Ground was good to walk on little slippy in places so some good footwear is recommended.

reviews.author: Mathew Bourne
reviews.text: Stang Forest has two small car parks, bonus is that parking is free. I chose the higher car park to explore the forest and the hills above, but plenty of paths throughout the forest to choose from, but do take a map if new to the area to ensure you find your way back. There is also extensive forestry work ongoing at the minute but plenty of warning signs to keep you safe. Area great for wildlife such as Curlew, finches, buzzards, lapwings, oystercatcher, plus the odd deer spotted as well.

reviews.author: Abbi Adair

Such a lovely walk through the woods & down onto the estate if you park near the red church off the main road.
You can completely walk all the way around the lake & get some really stunning photos.
Well worth the visit & just a short drive off the A76.

Beautiful walks and stunning scenery, playground for youngsters, good parking facilities. Saddened to see many parts of the forest strewn with filled dog waste bags. It would be better to use a stick to flick it off the paths where it can biodegrade far more quickly than in a plastic bag or simply take it home…

There is a lovely loch (loch Plantane) in the Center with a walk that goes right around the loch. The loch has a few benches to sit and view the beautiful scenery. The hungry ducks 🦆 will come right out of the wa

reviews.author: Eilidh Black
reviews.text: The paths here are very high quality and don't disturb the beautiful nature. This place is just beautiful. We walked around the loch, which was the perfect length for us. There are a couple of sculptures. It's quite a popular place for dog walking. There are longer paths if you prefer. The large car park is not technically free but is so cheap it may as well be. No overnight parking. Take note there are no bins here so take your rubbish (and poo bags!) home.

Alive with amazing fungi.  Merlin bird app reported crossbills & redwings which I could hear but failed to spot in the tall conifers.  Need to return as I know I have much more to discover & enjoy - as reserve is on both sides of road.  Fabulous village shop in Graffham with excellent coffee, hot snacks & WC!

reviews.text:
Treasure of a place and can't believe it has taken 35 years to find it.

reviews.author: Andrew Thomas
reviews.text: Beautiful area of chalk downland with wooded valleys and open hills offering stunning views over the village of South Harting and further over West Sussex, Hampshire and the Isle of Wight. On a clear day Portsmouth is visible from the highest summit. Paths are well marked and signposted but retain a pleasantly natural feel. There's a good size parking area free for National Trust members, though no other on-site facilities. Some of the hills are a steep climb but worth the effort for the views.

reviews.author: SID

Also hosts the biggest Go Ape raised obstacle course in the UK.

It’s perfect for long walks or bike rides, you can hire both standard bikes and e-bikes on site.

I went there for its connection to War of the Worlds..
It was good to see various references to it from an enthusiasts point of view.
I followed the 2 hour walk around Woking and the common.

You only get 2 hours in the common car park. I parked in the street in another road near the car park.
Don't forget to see the fighting machine and more on the 2 hour walk..

places_data.editorial_summary: Many Royal Navy ships were built with wood from this forest with cycle hire facilities & a cafe.

reviews.text:
Fantastic place to walk the dog/dogs. I highly recommend Alice Holt. There are plenty of walking paths, and cycle paths for all abilities. If you are more adventurous Go Ape is also available here, for tree top fun.

For children there are play parks and the Gruffalo walk which includes beautiful carved caricatures from the books.

There is also a lovely cafe on site.

reviews.author: Phil Sharp
reviews.text:
Family trip in February. Car parking is well organised but not cheap. Great trails and plenty of exploring possible. Facilities are excellent, even with a dog wash station if that’s something for you!

Play areas are very good and entertained the children for a good while.

Highly recommend this place. Best time is later in the afternoon to miss the crowds.

Towards the end of our walk there was a field with a shelter in just before we got to the forest. reviews.author: Gareth Keyes
reviews.text:
Regularly walk the dog here, absolutely great spot!

Lots of different routes around the forest to walk or ride. Expect to see lots of dog walkers and the odd horse or two. Great in both summer and winter.

reviews.author: Mike Parker

reviews.author: Doug Redmond

reviews.author: Stephen Gray
reviews.text:
Nice place to go for a walk with nice trees and plenty of paths to walk down.
There is also plenty of wildlife to see such as birds and insects.
The only downside is that people dont clear up after their dog and there are a lot of dog walkers around the carpark area.
However, if you go to the bottom end of the wood then it is nice and quiet.

reviews.author: Mark Westwood
reviews.text: My partner and I recently counted 75 dog shits on the path in the first 100m of the track leading up from the car park. Once you are through the shit zone the wood is actually very pleasant.

reviews.author: Colin Winn
reviews.text: A wonderful place to walk, either with your dog or not. Miles of paths and tracks, some really suited to wheelchairs/pushchairs. The wood is large enough for you to feel like you are the only person there, and if you are lucky you may spot some of the deer and other wildlife that live there.

reviews.author: Hayley clayton

reviews.author: Paul Davies

Wakerly is better, in my opinion, on all counts; while Wakerly is great for bikes, we saw only 2, and the riders were considerate and courteous. reviews.author: Phiona Richards

There are many trails through the woods, but quite often these are now claimed by these two wheeled, two legged enthusiasts. Most are very polite, but you do get an occasional one who thinks they own the woods.

reviews.author: Nick Harker

I have been visiting here for over 5 years, enjoying the  walks.  It’s beautiful, miles of well signposted trails for short walks or longer treks.
The annual parking pass is reasonably priced and well worth the investment, as you can’t get bored of the peaceful environment.
The cafe offers good food and drinks, at a fair cost. In summer time there is also a nice outdoor seating area.
I’ve hired bikes and the staff were helpful and it added to a day out.
Toilets are clean and easy to access.

Highly recommended for an afternoon’s walk.

Most of the pathways are easy, but mind that near Harbour Hill tracks may not be visible due to boggy conditions. Either dare climb to the top (421m) or walk around it. Maps can be found on the Pentland Hills website: https://www.pentlandhills.org/downloads/download/1/pentland-hills-visitor-guide-and-map

Very  nice  experience.  I recommend a visit here to enjoy the peace of the nature.

reviews.text: Went for a lovely evening walk.  Sadly, I did not see the red squirrels. However, there were birds, and also saw the tiniest frogs I have ever seen.

reviews.author: Geba Cowa
reviews.text: This walk was really lovely. The path was a bit overgrown in parts, but still obvious and doable. You need a reasonable level of fitness for it. The trail was undulating. It follows the Clyde River which is silent at times and has rapids at others. There are some very steep drops beside the path. There are warning signs. We felt quite safe. Children should be warned and dogs controlled in that section of the walk.

reviews.author: Caledonia Wanderer
reviews.text: Great local walk. A regular route for me that is very enjoyable and not too difficult in terms of terrain and level changes. Can be added on to a longer walk up through Cleghorn Glen, ending up at the road bridge over the River Mouse near the village of Cleghorn. The route can also be walked in reverse and always gives fine views. Plenty of wildlife can be encountered and I've never found the path to be heavily used or busy. Highly recommended!!

reviews.author: Alasdair MacDonald
reviews.text: Fantastic wee walk lasting about an hour, from the blue bridge over the mouse(glenbirnie/woodend) all the way to cartland bridge. Mature woodland walk with some great views looking towards Lanark (mousebank rd area). Lots of birdlife and possibly deer too. Some high crags so wouldn't recommend going off piste lol. The initial hill is quite steep too.

reviews.text: This is literally my daughter's favourite place in Essex. It has incredible walks and loads of green areas. The air is clean and you get to see a bit of wildlife too. You can have a snack at the cafe and even purchase many wonderful Julia Donaldson books and merchandise. 100% would recommend! ❤️

reviews.author: Rosamund Malick
reviews.text: It was a short journey from where we lived to get to this spacious and peaceful park. There are lots of trails, benches and the lake is very pretty. There is plenty of parking space on a Sunday and a handy map near the entrance to help you decide on a route. Quite a few families had organised picnic provisions to spend a good few hours and could watch their kids running around from deck chairs they’d bought with them.

reviews.author: Robert Walton
reviews.text: This afternoon, walking in Harwood Dale Forest to Penny Howe. Around here there was ritual & funerary activity during the prehistoric period, so there is a dystopian air in the landscape. No-one else around... we were alone! Parking in lay-bys off the A171, but no proper car park or toilets. Forest tracks are firmly stoned and easy to walk. Off the tracks, though, it can be muddy, so take care!

🚴👍

Nice walk, could be better signposted but it is what it is.

Some fab views of whitby and the Abbey though 😍😍

I'd definitely suggest heading left once you cross the bridge as the path here is in a much better state of repair. It was lovely to listen to the birds singing but just couldn't relax and enjoy the walk due to the dangers underfoot so was left a bit disappointed. Would be lovely on a warm, dry day!

A gorgeous part of the world and lucky to belong to everyone!

There are several trails varying in length to explore, a very decent visitor's centre, good parking (only £6 for the day), some activities for children to partake in and the odd medieval festival. A small square of shops preceeds the way to the forest with some unique ones among them.

Note that very near the forest is Edwinstowe

reviews.author: Luke Garratt
reviews.text: These woods are beautiful! A lovely, quiet place to explore and pass a few hours with your friends or by yourself. The carpark is a decent size, but watch the speed bumps as you enter as they can catch you out if you have a lower car like myself. All in all, a beautiful place that you should definitely visit sometime.

reviews.author: Kelly Smith
reviews.text: We stayed for 2 nights in the lakeview pod vip at haywoods and thoroughly enjoyed our stay. We were welcomed to check in early by phonecall which was nice and the reception staff were lovely and friendly when we arrived. The pod itself was clean and as expected. There was a lovely welcome gift of coffees, teas, biscuits and crackers. The fridge was stocked with water, fruit shoots, jam, cheese and milk all included which was a lovely touch. The pod came with bath robes, ideal for getting in and

reviews.author: Jordan Holmes
reviews.text:
Had no issues!
First time here, would definitely come again.
Nice cosy cabin, clean and loved the hot tub.
Had a great stay.

reviews.author: wyattap
reviews.text:
Experience is over and done with in a blink of an eye, but worth every penny.
For those who havnt shot, or even the profound, this is one for you. And when it ends, you can buy yourself membership, and go again when ever you like.

reviews.author: Martin Winfield
reviews.text: Lovely place to go and have a peaceful walk.

reviews.author: Hayley Winfield
reviews.text: Lovely place for a family walk with a four legged friend.

reviews.author: robin curwood
reviews.text: Nice place to walk and listen to the birds. 😊

reviews.author: Nigel Sarsfield
reviews.text: Blidworth Woods is a lovely peaceful area to visit and walk around. There are lots of paths and places to explore. It is possible to do a circular walk from the top car park to the bottom car park. It is worth using a map as navigating the large amounts of paths can be challenging. There is a central path which just runs straight through the wood from the car parks. Car parks are currently free to use and there are plenty of parking spaces.

reviews.author: Mohamed Mohamed
reviews.text: Blidworth Woods is a peaceful and scenic forest area perfect for a walk or a nature retreat, but visitors should be cautious as there is no official map or clear signposts, making it easy to lose your way if you're unfamiliar with the area. Despite this, it's a pleasant spot with useful amenities: there’s a free car park available, which makes access convenient. You’ll also find restroom facilities on-site and a small place offering snacks or light refreshments, making it a decent stop for famil

reviews.author: Kay Brown
reviews.text:
Lots of paths to walk, some steeper and rougher underfoot than others. Be prepared to either "stick and flick" or take your bagged doggy poo home with you are there are no bins.
The mobile cafe is wonderful and serves a selection of human treats but more importantly has dog sausages.

reviews.author: Jakub Sloma
reviews.text: Had a great experience walking around the forest in this amazing weather absolutely amazing scenery and a very big walk for any active dog loads of trails and even a pond at the car park. Would 100% recommend to any dog walker or anyone looking to go for a walk if you are near by or looking for a walking spot for either your dog or you

reviews.author: Vik Z
reviews.text: It's a lovely woodland but I wasn't feeling it. It was not a relaxing walk for me. The place is beautiful, there are woodland parts as well as little fields. Most of the paths double up as bridleways too. We didn't see any horse riders but you can see they've been used for riding horses regularly 😌. You can also park for free at the side of the road which is an added bonus.

reviews.author: G
reviews.text: Beautiful peace and quiet in abundance here, with lovely paths to take the dog walking. Walked adjacent to the old railway line.

places_data.address: Broad Dr, Mansfield NG21, UK, UK

reviews.author: Shaun
reviews.text: Had another lovely walk here. Such a lovely place for nature. And Such a beautiful place to visit. Stepped in history for you to discover. I guarantee a great day come rain or shine.

reviews.author: Jeroen van Drongelen
reviews.text:
As you would expect from such a famous and legendary place, this forestland is very well kept and quite a sight to behold because of the diversity and age of the trees. There are several trails varying in length to explore, a very decent visitor's centre, good parking (only £6 for the day), some activities for children to partake in and the odd medieval festival. A small square of shops preceeds the way to the forest with some unique ones among them.

Note that very near the forest is Edwinstowe

reviews.author: James Rose
reviews.text: Always enjoy our trips here! great walks and wonderful for the dog to walk too, Kids always enjoy it. I feel a few more bits scattered about for kids would be good but otherwise makes for a nice peaceful day out!

tags.fixme: This large area needs breaking down into managable smaller areas to allow more specific tagging
tags.name: Clipstone Forest

reviews.author: Rachel Turner
reviews.text: Big car park which was reasonably priced. Plenty of trails for walking and biking however they are not very well marked and it is quite easy to end up walking on a biking trail by accident. The area that they have set up about the trenches is very interesting and engaging.

reviews.author: Jessica Wells
reviews.text: Great place to go for a long walk or a bike ride! Saw all sorts of nice biking trails that makes me want some wheels.

reviews.author: David Burton
reviews.text: Why would you not come here?? It has so much to offer. You can either come spend a fortune renting bikes and completing go ape or bring a picnic your walking shoes and follow the trails including the Gruffalo trail. Lots of little play areas for kids. Lots of walking trails and areas for picnic and even quite secluded areas for a nice quite picnic. All day parking is only £6 aswell which is reasonable.

reviews.author: Kirsty Jackson
reviews.text: Always great adventures to be had here. There’s a main car park for free and various other places to park once you find your favourite spots. You can follow flat main tracks or go a bit off the beaten track. Whatever your walking style is there’s something for everyone.

reviews.author: Nathan Lilley
reviews.text:
A wonderful woods located just off the a46 near Newark, the woods is split in two half’s by a road. The centre of the woods hosts the car park that is free parking. Be aware there are no toilets or bins located here so you will have to be prepared.

The woods itself is a stunning place. One side has a long hard packed gravel path. the other is more trails than paths but still very lovely. You can expect to walk the whole thing in around 2 hours. Great location for dog walks,runs and apparently h

reviews.author: Ian Pascall
reviews.text: Perfect for walking your dog or dogs. Lots of trails into the woods or paths if that’s what you prefer.

tags.fixme: all tracks in forest need survey. some may be footpaths instead of tracks and vice verca. if you are really keen the flow of ditches need checking
tags.name: Parkhurst Forest

reviews.author: Donna W
reviews.text: Lovely forest with lots of trees, a few sculptures and a red squirrel hide. There's decent paths throughout, plus some paths that are more like mud tracks. There's also a free carpark.

reviews.author: Rafaela Moy
reviews.text: Fantastic magical forest on the Isle of Wight.

reviews.author: Simon Overton
reviews.text:
I visit most days to walk my dog. It is a beautiful forest. You might get to see the red squirrels if you are lucky.There is a large carpark and there are easy tracks to walk and for those more adventurous there are plenty of off the main route tracks. There is a purpose built squirrel hide that is well signposted from the main carpark where you will find the information board.
This forest is full of flora and fauna and has something to offer all year round.
There are no toilets here.

tags.description: Large woodland consisting of predominantly oak and douglas fir trees.Operated by the Friends of Bentley Wood upon previous purchase from the Forestry Commission, and an SSSI (Nature reserve)
tags.name: Bentley Wood

reviews.author: Katy
reviews.text: This is one of my favourite places to walk in the surrounding areas of Salisbury. Its huge, so you could spend 3 hours walking, or follow the maps and do a 30 min loop. Great for dog walking but be aware of the deer.

reviews.author: Clive Cheeseman
reviews.text: The best woodland I've ever seen. So beautiful at any time of year. Early April now, so large areas are covered in bluebells. Many deer set it off beautifully, but try to be quiet if you want to see them.

reviews.author: Rachel Harris-Jones
reviews.text: We come here for its beautiful running and walking routes. It also is great if you like to spot deer.

places_data.address: New Road, Landford, Salibury SP5 2AZ, UK

reviews.author: Jonathan Banks
reviews.text: Good secure stud, well run

tags.description: Large private oak woodland owned by the Langley Estate that is designated as a National Nature Reserve.Used for shooting deer.
tags.name: Langley Wood

reviews.author: Daniel Rimer
reviews.text:
Lovely nature reserve with well signed paths for walking. There's a tiny car park for up to 3-4 cars so access is limited.
I knocked off one star since the path is for walkers only. It would be fantastic to ride a bike around the path and would also mean that one wouldn't need to drive there, reducing the need for parking.

reviews.author: Bryn Webber
reviews.text: Good place to walk the dogs. Circle walk part-gravelled but second half can be muddy.

reviews.author: julie fleat
reviews.text: Great for dog walks.

reviews.author: Chris Bilson
reviews.text: Fantastic place to start a long hike around the many hard standing paths. Idea area all year round for a walk. Lots of little side tracks for dry months. You can get up to Andrew's Mare to see the ponies and loop back down to the fireplace which is about 7 miles. Lovely place to visit earlier in the day.

reviews.author: John Calvert
reviews.text: Lovely place to walk , anytime of the year 😃

reviews.author: Khánh Tín Nguyễn
reviews.text: This is truly a place you should visit at least once when in the UK. We had a short 3-day cycling trip here. Everything was wonderful — from the scenery to the people. Just a note: you should prepare an offline map before coming, as in some areas the signal is very weak and you can hardly use online maps.

reviews.author: Gaming Account
reviews.text: Had a beautiful hike close to the New Forest Inn Lyndhurst. Highly recommend. It was quiet, beautiful and spacious.

reviews.author: Robert Andrlik
reviews.text:
When the sun dips behind the trees and the light softens, the New Forest becomes pure magic. Walking along winding paths — sometimes straight through the woods — feels effortless with the fresh scent of nature all around.

25 km passed without me even noticing, just chilling, absorbing the atmosphere, and enjoying the calm. Perfect time of day to visit if you want peace, beauty, and that grounding forest energy.

⭐️ Highly recommend: come in the late afternoon, slow down, and let the forest do t

reviews.author: Martyn Donlon
reviews.text: Lovely fresh morning walk, too cold for mud, hurrah, no dirty puppies!!! Views not too good though!

reviews.author: Jay Bennett
reviews.text: Came here on a foggy day and it felt magical. Such a beautiful walk

reviews.author: Billy Potts
reviews.text: Brilliant walk with a free car park, only came across 2 othere people during our walk, surprisingly really as the car park was packed. Very peaceful and quiet, no litter around for the dog to sniff but definitely a wellys walk.

reviews.text: Nice place just to get away if you can find your way around (as the crow flys ;))

reviews.author: J. H.
reviews.text: Amazing in spring-there are so many bluebells 😊

reviews.author: Jayne Wyn
reviews.text: Beautiful when bluebells are out....nice track to walk on that belongs to a local estate and you can get to Farley church and Farley Mount easily along the paths. Lots of dog walkers about and horses.

reviews.author: Martyn Lloyd
reviews.text: Probably didn't go at the best time of year (early March) as no flowers out, trees bare, and very muddy! So not very picturesque. But main reasons for the poor score is that unaware woods are private land and you had to stick to paths and keep dogs on a lead. Which is not what we wanted or why we went there.

tags.description: A large woodland managed by the Forestry Commission ,with predominantly beech woodland mixed in with cedar and douglas fir plantations.Designated for public access.

reviews.author: Vince Eveleigh
reviews.text: If you get the opportunity this is a must walk in the most fabulous scenery. The trees and the landscape are simply awesome. It's a good solid route to walk, or cycle or whatever you choose. A great circular hike that I highly recommend

reviews.author: Andrew ‘Bear’ Jones
reviews.text: Amazing, full of nature, not many people, picturesque and so magical. Take my dog there at least 2 times a week and she loves it.

reviews.author: Mike B
reviews.text: I enjoy coming here for a stroll with new forest ponies roaming freely and for the runners out there it’s also a great place for a park run

reviews.author: Tammy Jones
reviews.text: Lovely woodland walks with lots of ponies roaming about. Good place to take the dog off the lead but beware of deers! Easy parking and free to park.

reviews.author: Liz Mitchener
reviews.text: Easy parking, Great mix of walks, you can spend a couple of hours in the woods asmiring mature oak and beech, then cross into the heath. Benches and tables round the edges. Choice of easy access man made paths suit prans/ mobility scooters or go off piste . Excellent habitat to explore with flaura and fauna both large and small.

It was an unusually sunny day in February, so we were spoiled with the warmth and sun creeping through the trees. It was beautiful. We walked a two mile route, which took a couple of hours, as the children (5 next month and 8 and a half) loved finding dens, balancing on logs and exploring. We took lunch and ate in the peaceful

reviews.author: Igor Iena
reviews.text: Parking is free and there are always free spaces. Unfortunately, there is a lot of garbage nearby. In 5 minutes there is wonderful nature, many mushrooms, berries, you can take a photo next to the horses walking in the forest. Advice, if you turn from the paths, take boots with you, it is quite wet everywhere.

reviews.author: J F
reviews.text: Beautiful place for a walk, large forest area's around small car park. It's definitely a much muddier area, I can imagine after a long rainfall, it being a very hard going place for walking.

reviews.author: Len Underwood
reviews.text: So this place is beautiful. There's some really lovely dense forest to be explored. Also plenty of clear and easy pathways to walk if your feeling slightly more casual. An excellent venue for a dog walk or bike ride. Be advised during wetter weather the paths can become treacherous. Bring wellies

reviews.author: Dawn Pine (Dawnie)
reviews.text: Highly recommended for anyone needing to get out and be surrounded by nature. Definitely wear walking boots/wellies where it's very boggy/muddy but still great fun to walk around. It massively helped me today where I've been recovering from being so overloaded/overwhelmed recently. Really tranquil and beautiful to listen to & identify various bird species too. The New Forest is always a beautiful place to visit 💚

reviews.author: Linda Pullan
reviews.text:
I found Godshill Inclosure by accident and so glad I did. It's a beautiful spot for a picnic. There are also some lovely walks to be had in the woods.

Parking is easy. Entrance and parking are free.

Dogs should be kept under control, ideally on a lead as there are New Forest ponies around.

The ground is fairly even if you have mobility issues. But please note there are no public toilets.

Just remember not to pet or feed the ponies and donkeys that roam freely in the New Forest

reviews.author: David Smith
reviews.text:
There are two elements to this area, firstly the enclosure. Good parking with no charge, generally all the walking areas are quite flat. The  woods encompass a wide range of wildlife with various routes to take. It is quite easy to lose your bearings but Google maps works quite well to guide you back to the carpark. Just outside of the carpark is a great area for a picnic.

The second place to go is to the viewing point, this looks out over the countryside with a spectacular view.

places_data.address: B3056, Beaulieu Rd, Southampton SO40 7AA, UK

reviews.author: Mark Marshall
reviews.text: As with all inclosures, there is forest, and heathland. This one also has a boggy marsh - we visited on a rainy day in May so the bog was more boggy than normal but it didn't stop us enjoying the tranquility, birdsong and marsh flowers. Wandering ponies were an added bonus!

reviews.author: Felicity
reviews.text:
Perfect stop to jump onto the New Forest cycleway with easy access to Lyndhurst or Brockenhurst.

Well laid paths suitable for walking or cycling and child friendly.

reviews.author: martin rain
reviews.text: Lovely walk with big open spaces and wood walks of track.

reviews.author: Jeannie Cherry
reviews.text: The variety of experiences within the forest makes it appealing. Tranquil walks to soak in the natural beauty of the area, cycling and running are complemented by more developed park areas that cater for all ages.

reviews.author: Malcolm Wilcox
reviews.text:
Peaceful and relaxing forest for a walk of various lengths. Good for dogs, although paths come close to fast roads on a couple of occasions so beware if your dog is off the lead.
Ebblake north car park is a good place to park

reviews.author: Lale Ibadula
reviews.text: Peaceful and beautiful place to spend time in nature.

reviews.author: Marian Croitoru
reviews.text:
If you’re looking for a peaceful escape into the heart of the New Forest, the Standing Hat car park inclosure is a perfect starting point. It’s one of those spots that feels tucked away from the main tourist hubs, making it ideal for a relaxed day out with the family.
​The trails here are what make it special. They are wide, well-maintained, and relatively flat, so you don’t need to be a professional hiker to enjoy them. Whether you’re on foot or bringing the bikes, the scenery is stunning, with

reviews.author: adrian day
reviews.text: Nice walk through woodland mostly on gravel track. Several paths you can follow.

reviews.author: Ian Chamberlain
reviews.text: I walked from Lyndhurst and stopped here for something to eat in the car park before walking through Holland's Wood, King's Hat and back to the Crown Stirrup pub. It was a fairly long walk on one of the best days of the year so far. It was sunny, and dry and so were the pathways. I will definitely be back. The video was taken at Parkhill Inclosure. Hopefully more photos to follow. I had a great day here.

reviews.author: Alan Sutton
reviews.text: Great walk.  Lots to see especially away from the main path.

reviews.author: Anne-Marie KW
reviews.text: Lovely wild location. Encounters with ponies pretty much guaranteed. It's quite a samey walk around the perimeter, local woodland more beautiful but worth a visit

reviews.author: DashCam UK
reviews.text: Lovely walk, car park is full of pot holes so would suit a 4x4. Area also prone to flooding but as long as you wear walking type shoes you will be fine

reviews.author: Hilary Ball
reviews.text: Lovely walk mainly on gravel paths. Big car park.

reviews.author: Mike
reviews.text:
Lovely wooded area to have a walk around .

Alot of horses to see on the outside of the woods .

Good parking around the area.

Ideal place to take the dogs for a walk .

Everyone we walked past seemed friendly and said hello and stopped to talk aswell.

reviews.author: Robert Andrlik
reviews.text: It’s one of many entries into the New Forest, with easy access to the enclosure paved with solid gravel paths as well as opportunities to explore the woods throughout off track adding great benefit to trail running

reviews.author: Fr K
reviews.text: Holmsley and the surrounding area of the New Forest is just beautiful.  I lived nearby for several years and miss its quiet beauty. Loads of free parking dotted about and trails to follow. If you have a dog then it's a paradise for them to explore.

reviews.author: John Watton
reviews.text: Small gravel New Forest car park. Access to a cycle path along old railway line and walks along Holmsley Ridge. The road to the car park is narrow and quite steep and not suitable for larger vehicles such as motorhomes or commercial vehicles.

reviews.author: Steve Smith
reviews.text: Busy and badly parked drivers

reviews.author: Sam Moz
reviews.text:
This was our local walk while I was away with my dog.  The pathways were great so even on a rainy day we could still get out and about.

The little fairy doors were cute and a nice little find.

The car park is free.

reviews.author: Andrew Cartwright
reviews.text:
We entered the forest via Shorefield holiday park, it was a really nice walk - just a little disappointed that some dog owners can not pick up after their dogs.

We took a buggy for the really little person and we did OK, albeit a bit bumpy in places!

reviews.author: Martin Winterburn
reviews.text: One of my favourite places to run .. love it here , also nice for a bike ride or walk with the kids

reviews.author: Genevieve Matthews
reviews.text: Wonderful atmosphere, I don't usually like pine forests but this was different, interesting walks but we missed the biggest waterfall because signs didn't show it as there had been a landslide. Lots of picnic benches overlooking a quaint river, the landscape continues to change throughout the walk. We all loved it.

reviews.author: Elaine Henderson
reviews.text: Very peaceful, lots of birdsong. Good noticeboard. Take your binoculars. Found two plastic bottles- please don't leave rubbish here!

reviews.author: Sammi Ross
reviews.text: Went in 2022, will return here in summer. Very much enjoyed lovely peaceful place, Plenty paths to follow

reviews.text: Excellent for hiking and dog walk

reviews.text: Great walking & cycling

reviews.author: Stephanie Featherstone
reviews.text: The owners went above and beyond to make sure I was well fed, comfortable and looked after, after long days walking. They picked me up from the trail and returned me the next day to avoid walking on the A82, and the food was homemade and delicious. They have something special here. Recommend in the highest terms.

reviews.author: hjalmar fossi
reviews.text: Lovely couple of people running this place! The room was clean and quiet. The food was good and it should be noted that they offered a dinner as well during my stay. If you are on foot, there is a short walk along a pretty busy roadway.

reviews.author: Tony O'Sullivan
reviews.text: This is a lovely little guest house. Lorraine and Laura are super hosts, the rooms they provide are wonderfully inviting after a long day's walking,  and the food on offer is excellent. What made the stay special was the chance to sit and chat with this lovely couple. Nothing was too much trouble for them. I recommend a stay here without reservation  - and do try the home made ginger cake, it's delicious.

reviews.author: Andy P
reviews.text: Highy recommended. Some walks are challenging but spectacular scenery. Very well maintained and dog friendly.  Well worth a visit.

reviews.author: Peter Williams
reviews.text: Relaxing location. Wonderful location to de-stress.

reviews.author: Rachel

reviews.author: Sam Stone
reviews.text:
Amazing scenery!

Free parking (although not loads so imagine on a busy day could be an issue)

The shorter boardwalk path is really pretty and nice and easy. We also did the blue walk which was longer but very scenic. Not too difficult and mostly flat but also had some slopes up and down.

There are only portaloos at the car park but there is quite a few of them and they were reasonably clean (had paper but no hand sanitizer)

Really lovely walks and I would recommend it.

reviews.author: K Rae
reviews.text:
Pretty forest with lots of great riverside views and a few nice cascades. Well marked paths leading up out of the forest to the source of the river Severn, and access to two long distance paths.
The TikTok famous raised boardwalk is all of a ten minutes maximum, and the accessible paths are less than a mile long. If this area is local to you then it's a beautiful place, if it requires a bit of travel then there really isn't anything here that likely isn't already more local to you, and I wouldn'

reviews.author: Anita
reviews.text:
Beautiful autumnal walk for a few hours following the red, blue then yellow routes. All very well signposted too.

Key points:
• Dog friendly which is always a bonus for us.
• The boardwalk is stunning but only around 30 mins in duration, if you require accessible paths this may be disappointing.
• We arrived early as staying in the local area so was very peaceful but definitely gets busier in to midday time!
• Rhyd-y-benwch carpark is free but tiny and currently has portaloos.

But the trees are tall and some older specimens with large diameter trunks have been retained, particularly near the trails. Some open area, but trails are mostly through canopied forest.

reviews.author: Elle Simpson
reviews.text:
What a beautiful location and so full of history from a Pictish hill fort with spectacular views over loch tummel to the snow topped mountains in the distance to the remains of a 300 year old village hidden away in magical woods
Some great view points and a few hidden totem poles to find along the walking routes and the paths are great, parking was free and there are compos ting toilets available
I would highly recommend a visit here

reviews.author: David Cameron

reviews.author: Munro Kerr
reviews.text: A very under appreciated and magnificent forest, teeming with rare species of flora and fauna. One of the greatest forests within the UK by far spanning this lovely area of the Tayvalley and towards Blair Atholl.

reviews.author: Mike Cochrane

Pembrey Forest is a large sprawling forest in the South Wales coast in Pembrey. The forest it’s self is full of history with signs of the world wars with the pillboxes and remains of the Pembrey munitions factory.

There are many paths and trails you can walk ranging in length with plenty of wild life from Birds to fish in the lakes. Over all it’s very much worth a visit.

Some days can be noisy in the northeast corner due to racing track near airfield. Has a few places with picknick benches. Various types of forest as rewilding allowed and managed but sections still evergreen.

reviews.author: Neil Richards

Dogs can shit in the woods, but people with van can't park as it's assumed they are campers who will leave a mess ?

reviews.text:
Beautiful, peaceful woods. Ideal for an easy and relaxing walk. We heard quite a few birds and saw different types of mushrooms. The trails were really well sign posted.

We did find a few bits of rubbish on and off trail, so a reminder to everyone to not only take their trash home but also to pick up other people's when they can so it doesn't harm wildlife.

reviews.author: Gareth Eccles
reviews.text: Good parking and a great place to go for a walk with or without a dog. Have tried the sculpture trail but sadly there appears to be a few pieces missing, the trail is also poorly sign posted so you can lose the trail quite easily. Add in the fact that the trails aren't maintained and it gets difficult in a few places. Other than that, it's got some great walks.

reviews.author: Phil Temple
reviews.text: Always go for walks here. Such a peaceful place early in the morning. You can almost be alone. The sun is just warming and the animals are awake and ready for the day.  Strolling through masses of bluebells, fir trees, major oaks and felling zones. Plenty to see for all ages. A great and friendly woodland.

reviews.author: Ian
reviews.text: Beautiful pristine woods with a nice selection of trees and a really nice to place to explore. Perfect place for walking the dog. There are no bins so if you can’t be bothered to take your rubbish home or think it’s ok to litter then please do not visit.

reviews.author: Francesca Partridge
reviews.text: This is a beautiful wood with reasonable paths and seats to admire the views. Bonsai Bank is a must for butterfly and orchid lovers with a colony of Duke of Burgundy  butterflies as well as a superb display of lady orchids.  Care must be taken to stick to the paths and not damage this wonderful site. This is a Kent wildlife Trust reserve.

reviews.author: Toby Morgan

Apart from bisons you can see other interesting grazer like Exmoore ponies, Iron Age pigs and longhorn cattle. The non native pine plantations are slowly being removed and coppicing takes place in encourage wildlife such as the heath fritilary butterfly and nightingales. The moss was like a soft green blanket covering everywhere. Great for dog walking. Free parking area.

reviews.author: E Shilland
reviews.text:
Dealing with Logie Timber for a small job bisecting and angling the ends of 1200 tree stakes was a pleasure from start to finish.

After a quick call, Angus provided a very reasonable quote by email within 20 minutes. Arriving the next day, Angus himself helped unload the wood from the van and the job was done within 24 hours. The office staff were extremely pleasant and helpful, and invoicing/payment was quick and easy.

Absolutely recommend.

reviews.text:
Dyfi is the best place for gravity mountain biking in the UK. Most UK bike parks lack elevation and have to cram a lot into short, slow runs. Dyfi doesn't have that problem, so it's one of very few places you can really let rip on a downhill bike. Though that's not to say you can't have a good time on a trail bike too, and there are plenty of smaller features to play on.

The trails are world class, with a good blend of tech and flow, and run well in all conditions. It's not for absolute beginne

Cafe and visitor centre currently closed, but carpark, bike trails, bike shop and toilets/showers are open.
I was here to ride my bike on a very rainy day. Trails were in good condition. All stone, so no mud.
After my ride I was able to clean my bike with the free bikewash and got a 3min hot shower for just £1.
Great venue.

reviews.author: Maureen Leonard
reviews.text: We took the hardest walk with our dog and enjoyed the lovely surroundings and the waterfalls mid way and at the top. Not as spectacular as some as it’s a hydro plant, but still nice to look at. It took us a little over 2 hours to do and we are 60 and 64 but very active.  Some slippy points on the way back (large pebbles and tree roots) and is very steep at times so ensure you wear appropriate footwear and bring lots of water to drink!

reviews.author: Jon Dando

Well worth a visit and we’ll be back to continue exploring the many options!

reviews.author: Mark Stothard MA ARPS
reviews.text: Horner Water flows through Horner Woods on Exmoor, slipping around mossed stones beneath a dense green canopy. A fallen tree bridges the stream, slowing the eye and shaping the frame. Long exposure softens the current, revealing movement, depth, and the quiet persistence of water in an ancient woodland setting.

The Errwood and Fernilee reservoirs are prominent landmarks within the valley, with remains of an industrial past and ruins of the once grand manor house of Errwood Hall still visible in the area today. These echoes of the past create an atmosphere that you have to experience first hand to appreciate.

Cairns have been used as landmarks, trail markers to guide travellers, as burial monuments, ceremonial purposes, for astrology and even lunar calendars.

reviews.text: Decent carpark big woods a little river runs through it but there ain.t any seating areas or bench's on the pathways for resting could.nt brew up it says no stoves etc but we did a brew with a bcb on the carpark still a good place to spend the day its not far from trimpley reservoir an the Severn river

reviews.author: Katherine Russell
reviews.text: Lovely area to walk, easy to extend your walk to Trimpley, Arley or Habberley too. Well signposted, main paths as well as thinner less used paths, a field you can walk through with horses in, a few streams running towards the river too.

reviews.author: Trevor Stevens
reviews.text: Peaceful , fresh and great place for a run or MTB deserves to be kept niche

reviews.author: Ann Roberts
reviews.text: Great place for walking, has a little brook running through the woods. Ribbesford Church is worth seeing too, one of the paths runs straight through the graveyard

reviews.author: SAB photography
reviews.text: Lovely silent place to wander

reviews.author: Sassenach David
reviews.text: This place has been battered by storms and many areas are still closed off. The bits that are open are plastered with signs reminding you that the forest is doing its best to kill you. If you can get past that you can still have a decent stretch of the legs.

reviews.author: Souter Al
reviews.text:
A beautiful place that has been devastated by last year's storms. It looks like it going to be closed for work to be done for at least a year (now March 2022)
Save yourself the journey as the woods paths are completely blocked and impassable.
Not only have the Forestry Commission have the fallen trees to clear but also a survey will be needed on the damaged trees that are left.

reviews.author: Cameron Lewis
reviews.text: A lovely area of mixed woodland. Cracking views to the Cheviots.

reviews.author: Glenda McLellan
reviews.text: This is a private wood. Limited parking on edge of if the road. Good wood for spotting fungi

reviews.author: Ivan Cairns
reviews.text: Wonderfully secluded area. A sun trap indeed. And some classic climbs to boot!

reviews.author: Gemma Scougal
reviews.text: Great place for climbing although there was little breeze on a hot day. Nice routes.

reviews.author: Bridget Goffin
reviews.text: I love walking in Dunwich forest. There are loads of walks with differing landscapes. Coniferous, deciduous and mixed woodland, heathland and coastline. There is also a lovely cafe at The Dingles to take a break at.

reviews.author: Frances Exley
reviews.text:
A Godsend in the hot weather as we were able to enjoy the relative cool of the wooded areas in the recent heatwave. Our black labrador was most grateful too.
Lots of butterflies in the glades.
Also appreciate the sandy soil here especially when wet as it just brushes off boots and dog unlike the foul claggy clay at home.

reviews.author: B P
reviews.text: Lovely relaxing walk into the forest listening to the birds sing.

reviews.author: spiro d'manin
reviews.text:
I must say, Rendlesham forest did not disappoint, whilst wandering around the forest we did spot a strange looking craft in a clearing on one of the trails, see photo, it had some unusual markings reminiscent of graffiti often found on abandoned or vacant shop fronts we see.

reviews.author: Sarah Moulden
reviews.text: A large forest with marked paths in many directions, you can get lost in if you wander, which our children enjoyed figuring out which direction was the car park. Despite all the rain it wasn't very muddy. Definitely recommend.

Great MTB trail with exciting 'snake pit!'
Single track fun 10 mile ride.
Hard to find sangalls carpark for start ( it's off tunstall road). Very poor mobile reception makes hard to find car park

reviews.author: Robert Budd
reviews.text: A pleasant place to walk, cycle or ride a horse. Plenty of trails without having to cross busy roads.

reviews.author: William Hawkins
reviews.text: A lovely place to get away and take your dog out for a long walk.  You can park up follow the paths and walk for miles taking in all the nice surroundings, we even saw deer run past us.

reviews.author: Carl Scott
reviews.text:
Lovely walk, there is place for a few cars but the entrance is very uneven so would recommend a car with high clearance.

There is plenty of wildlife, although I didn't see any, there is ground sign of dear.

reviews.author: Paul S
reviews.text:
An absolutely beautiful forest to have a stroll in.  The man car park at Postern Hill has some picnic spots and free toilets - which (when I visited) were clean and very convenient.

It has an incredible abundance of beautiful trees and fauna with lovely clear laid out main paths.

Around the car park there were lots of people, and a lot of dog walkers, but the site is huge! Once you travel like 20 mins walk into the forest you’ll find yourself completely alone and it’s incredibly tranquil.

The

reviews.author: Derek
reviews.text: Went with grandchildren 8 & 10 yrs for enjoyable stroll through forest then back to picnic table for lunch.  Games then home. Very disappointed toilets were locked and there was no dog litter bin. The would area was very clean and a great free day out.

reviews.author: Shaun Mallett
reviews.text: This is a lovely serene place to walk and ponder. Lots of different things to look at (mainly trees obviously). Lots of different trails and paths to take. Have a go, it is very calming, you won't regret it.

tags.description: Mixed, private mainly oak woodland that is the second largest ancient woodland in Hampshire.Harewood Forest is used for both deer stalking and pheasant rearing and shooting.
tags.name: Harewood Forest

reviews.author: Paul Reynolds
reviews.text: Could be a great place like other woods in the county but sadly nearly all of it is fenced off with barbed wire and signs everywhere saying stay out. Such a shame, such a waste.

reviews.author: Daisy Young
reviews.text: A beautiful, quiet place that not many people know about since its tucked away on the side of a motorway. Lovely scenery and lot’s of space. From what i know of there’s not a car park, we parked on the pull over from the motorway, but if i am wrong please forgive me.

reviews.author: Mateusz Kazberuk
reviews.text: Fantastic place for a run

reviews.author: Andy StevensTV
reviews.text: Beautiful woodland area. Perfect for dog walks. Never busy. Bluebells are stunning as are all the flora and fauna. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Tahsin Ar Rahman
reviews.text: One of the best place for a family walk and looks beautiful when the blue bells are at full bloom. We were couple weeks early for the blue bells but definitely coming back later for them for some good clicks.

reviews.author: Andrea moulton
reviews.text: Lovely place to come for a walk, the trees here are quite old and tall and once the bluebells are out is a sea of colour. We were a bit early for this but 6 still got to see some interesting things.

places_data.address: Corncockle Estate, Templand, Lockerbie DG11 1EW, UK

reviews.author: A M
reviews.text: We had a fantastic stay here. The cabin was perfect for a long weekend of rest and relaxation. We loved watching the birds and red squirrels over breakfast and spending the evening in the hot tub with a glass of wine. The wood burning stove adds to the atmosphere but we also appreciated the electric heaters for early morning before we got the fire going! The surrounding grounds are beautiful for walks and the hosts were helpful, friendly and responsive if we had any queries. Highly reccomend.

reviews.author: Rick
reviews.text: What a gem, sitting having breakfast watching the red squirrel or soaking in the hot tub watching the colourful birds, stuck for choice!. Great location for visiting this great forgotten area of “Bonnie Scotland “. All topped off by your lovely hosts. Happy memories, happy days.

reviews.author: Tony McCall Dunlop
reviews.text: Loved this place, the owners are lovely, very helpful without being intrusive. The small touches really make the difference, fresh flowers in the room, bottle of milk in the fridge, plants on the step and the bird feeder outside the window, meant there were loads of tiny birds there all day. The stand out thing is the location, its secluded, private and quiet, nothing but the sound of birds. Perfect for a romantic getaway.

reviews.author: Lynne P . Humphreys
reviews.text: This is a lovely wood up above Carronbridge in Dumfries & Galloway. Its criss-crossed wirh logging trails so great for a good wander. We walked to Morton Castle which is about a 3 mile walk from the house we were staying in.

reviews.author: Kerry Thomson
reviews.text: Gorgeous walk and scenery

reviews.author: Eleanor Smith
reviews.text: Loads of beautiful walks on marked paths. Mixed woodland with lochs, burns, wildflowers.  If you are lucky you may spot deer, otters or kingfishers.

reviews.author: Al Alexander
reviews.text: Interesting to note that oak from this wood was purchased 1796 for construction of the Crinan canal (page 61 of The Crinan Canal by Marian Pallister)

reviews.author: Carolyn Butler
reviews.text: Beautiful place, definitely worth a visit you won't be disappointed

reviews.author: Jake B
reviews.text: Wonderful places to stop off when traveling up to the Highlands, toilets here are clean, free. Lots of branches to have picnics, car parking spaces and lots of walks going off. Clearly very well kept and maintained by the staff here. Just off the main road.

reviews.author: Sara Mitchell
reviews.text: Plotted a walk to see dam. Some bits of track are harder to follow but we made it up to the track. So quiet. We saw our first really wild red squirrel, so happy!

reviews.author: Craig Bromley
reviews.text: Contin forest walk has 2 paths. The first is a very short walk good for very small children with a place for a picnic and toilets. This is about 20min. This then leads to the second walk of around 7-8km taking 2 hours. This is a strenuous walk up hill to start over rocks and through woods. I think I missed the view rock at the top but still a few good places to see the surrounding hills. At places the route splits with signage showing other paths to join. Worth a trip for a family picnic or hike

places_data.address: Spital Wood, Muir of Ord IV6, UK

reviews.author: Matt Boxall
reviews.text: Nice woods with deer, red squirrels and lots of birds. Quite small and probably not a place to make a special visit to, however, enjoyable to walk around if you are in local accomodation.

reviews.author: Karen E.
reviews.text: Clear pathways, wide enough to keep dogs on the leash and away from all the ticks. Lots of beautiful butterflies, too! Local dog owners have contributed their time to providing bins for dog waste and thankfully (most) people seem to use them.  Was quite sheltered from the wind, too.

reviews.author: Stacey Dougal
reviews.text: Was recommended it as a nice place to walk the dog and it didn't disappoint. No waymarked paths as such, but we managed not to get lost and just enjoyed the peacefulness of the place. Was slightly muddy near the area of water (not sure if it's a marsh or a pond?) but the majority of the gravelled path was well kept and easy to walk on.

reviews.text: A lovely forest and Loch but forestry has felled large swathes, and fallen trees block the main paths in other areas making it pretty treacherous and unpleasant. Also very muddy in places. Such a shame as a bit of investment into clearing the paths and improving signage would make a huge difference. Please note if you are parking by the official Drimmie Wood entrance the path heads up the hill to the right BEFORE the gate, you don't need to go through the gate.

reviews.author: Drew Young

reviews.text:
A great circular walk round the reservoir and loads of smaller trails. Good for walking the dog but there are a lot of them and some owners are not very careful so we had a couple of incidents with our dog who was on a lead.

Good paths all round.

Loads of interest and lots for children to keep them interested also.

reviews.author: Malti A
reviews.text:
This is one of the best country parks, offering a lovely, long scenic trail walk. We visited on a sunny day, which made the experience even more enjoyable. Along the way, we had some small bites while taking in the beautiful greenery and peaceful surroundings.

It’s best to bring your own food and water, as there are some cafés and food trucks, but they were closed during our visit. The park features stunning valley views and charming bridges, making it a perfect spot to unwind and connect with

reviews.author: Travis Mager
reviews.text: A specter spot to visit and revisit. A long, dreamy locale in Fife that has a pine forest, rolling dunes, and a sprawling beach. Not terribly busy, but the car park can fill up. Perfect for a few hours of immersing yourself in big-sky nature.

reviews.author: Charmaine k
reviews.text:
I recommend taking a bus to Tayport instead of Morton Loch. You can take bus 42 Stagecoach from Dundee. Do check out their website for more information on how to get there

I really enjoyed the walk, beautiful birds and lochs. Would recommend downloading the map to see their walking trails.

reviews.author: William Shaw
reviews.text: Lovely serene woodland for a walk with well maintained trails. We actually accessed the woodland from parking on the south side of the A923, and then walking via Lairds Loch. Big loop of the woods takes roughly an hour.

reviews.author: Marcus Webster
reviews.text: I was disappointed to discover that this forest played no part in the battle that ended the Jacobite uprising. I would have expected that at least some woodland creatures loyal to the Jacobite cause could be shoe-horned into a Disney interpretation of the battle at Culloden so that American children could sing along with Bonnie Prince Charlie about the freedoms he hopes to bring to Scotland once free of the yoke of the beastly English.

reviews.author: Zsoka Simon
reviews.text: Magical ✨ place not too far from Inverness, one could expect a fairy would appear from behind the trees.. 😁 Absolutely stunning, the river, the trees, the moss, the mushrooms. Perfect spot to recharge your batteries and feel the power of nature. All the fallen trees are left to help the forest creatures reclaim their habitat. Autumn is magical here, so glad we came! Walking in the woods for over four hours seemed to be just enough to feel connected and refreshed. 🍂🌳

reviews.author: Coale
reviews.text:
My wife and I traveled to Scotland from the states last year for our honeymoon.  I had been to Scotland when I was a wee lad and remembered that there were these hidden little rain forest gems tucked away here and there.  I simply typed in "rain forest" into maps and this one popped up.  We were staying in Dornoch so it wasn't terribly far  (just beware of rush hour in Inverness which can be very slow).

The main hike is very easy.  However, there are other trails that are more of a challenge. M

reviews.author: Z VH

reviews.author: Geoff Dennis
reviews.text: When we visited the cafe was closed due to fire damage.  There is a large play area for children. The walks are beautiful and clean. The rapids and waterfalls are fantastic. We were out of season to see the jumping salmon. There is overnight parking for motorhomes, but it can work out expensive, £40 with the services. As noted below in fees, be careful of the 10 minutes to pay rule.

reviews.author: Old Age Traveller
reviews.text:
One of my favourite places to go for walk.
Quite and beautiful huge Douglas Fir trees.
Some of the tallest in Europe .
Don't get lost.

reviews.text: Take a break if you are in the area walking through the red woods is such a beautiful experience the tree are so awesome a must any day
